title: Certificate generation + public verification: server-gen number/code, publish→public_verification, revoke, real verify page
business_reason: SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP certificate_ops: 'no real generation (number/code/QR are dead helpers); public verify page is a static skeleton'. The certificates lifecycle (generate→published→revoked) is wired as bare status flips on core_certificates — nothing server-generates the credential identity or makes it publicly verifiable, and certificate creation can't even persist (verification_code is NOT NULL but absent from the createSchema → created_local). Wire the real generation: server-control certificate_number + verification_code, write the public_verification row on publish (so /verify works), reflect revoke, and ship a real public verify page.

design.server_gen_codes: computeOnCreate('certificates') -> certificate_number=CERT-XXXXXXXX + verification_code=VRS-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX (unguessable, server-controlled; overwrites any client value — P2.4/P3.9). Fixes create persistence (verification_code NOT NULL was unsupplied).
design.createschema_fix: core_certificates createSchema currently REQUIRES result_id + revocation_reason (conditional fields) -> broken create (§3.8). Require only student_id + school_id; result_id optional; certificate_number/verification_code server-gen (excluded from input).
design.publish_effect: kernel domain-effect hook (runDomainEffect, parallel to runTransitionEffect) -> server/certificates/certEffects.ts: on core_certificates:publish, upsert public_verification (verification_code, certificate_id, candidate_name from students.student_name lookup, status='valid', issued_on=today) + set certificates.issued_at; audited.
design.revoke_effect: on core_certificates:revoke, set public_verification.status='revoked' (so /verify shows revoked); audited.
design.verify_page: real public page app/verify (enter/auto-fill a verification code -> calls existing /api/verify/certificate/[code] -> shows valid/revoked + ONLY whitelisted fields + the QR/verify link). Replaces the static skeleton.

param_14.question: How is a certificate's identity server-generated and made publicly verifiable (and revocable), with a real verify page?
param_14.answer: On create, the server generates certificate_number + an unguessable verification_code (never client-typed). On publish, a domain effect writes a minimal public_verification row (whitelisted fields only) so the public /verify endpoint+page returns 'valid' with candidate/olympiad/award; on revoke it flips that row to 'revoked'. A real public verify page renders the whitelisted result. The existing signed-URL engine will carry the PDF when generation of the file lands (deferred).
param_14.answer_source: certificate_ops/workflows.json (certificate_lifecycle + verification_lifecycle), generation_policy/qr_policy/verification_policy, server/eval/certificate.ts, migration 0006 public_verification, core_certificates service
param_14.security_implications: verification_code is server-generated + unguessable (never client-supplied); /verify and public_verification expose ONLY whitelisted fields (candidate_name/olympiad_name/award/status/issued_on) — never PII/scores/ids; revoked certs publicly show 'revoked'; generation/publication/revocation are audited (hash-chained); rate-limited public endpoint already present.
param_14.approval_implications: publish/revoke remain approve-class transitions (reason required); no change.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: PDF generation + secure download of the certificate file reuses FR-SECURE-FILE-DOWNLOAD-0003 storeFile/signedUrl (certificates.pdf_file) — deferred follow-up. Reissue/supersede impact handling deferred.
param_14.assumptions: certificates.result_id is nullable in the live DB (seed inserts without it); public_verification (0006) exists; olympiad_name/award may be null until result join is added (candidate_name via students lookup is sufficient for the provable slice).
